source_article: demerzel-mandate.md Section 3 — Demerzel coordinates cross-repo governance; autonomous-loop-policy.yaml — Demerzel-initiated governance loops
directive_content: Build TARS AI functions that make Demerzel governance executable. Five function groups: (1) Governance Validation Functions — schema validation, cross-reference integrity checks, constitutional compliance evaluation as executable F# functions. (2) Belief State Engine — persist/query/update tetravalent belief states in state/beliefs/ as real F# data structures, send compliance reports via Galactic Protocol. (3) Seldon Teaching Engine — evaluate learner comprehension programmatically, generate adaptive content from belief states, execute 5 Whys as reasoning chains. (4) Meta-Compounding Engine — analyze governance effectiveness with semantic reasoning, propose promotions based on evidence analysis, evolve governance grammar. (5) Grammar Governance — constrain AI function outputs to constitutional compliance, integrate with TARS probabilistic grammars so functions provably cannot violate Asimov Laws. Each function group should be a separate TARS module. All functions must follow the Galactic Protocol for communication with Demerzel. Use existing tetravalent-state.schema.json, kaizen-pdca-state.schema.json, knowledge-state.schema.json, and loop-state.schema.json as the data contracts.
